title: [MEDIUM] Record epic creation and update activity events
description: Deep sweep finding: docs/SCHEMA.md and activity types include EPIC_CREATED and EPIC_UPDATED, and the UI has an ActivityLogPanel for them, but StateManager.createEpic and updateEpic only emit WebSocket events; they do not append EPIC_CREATED/EPIC_UPDATED to activity.log. Epic deletion is logged. This leaves audit history incomplete for epic lifecycle changes. Add activity logging for create/update with bounded payloads and tests.
definitionOfDone: StateManager.createEpic appends an EPIC_CREATED activity event with epicId/projectId and a bounded payload including title/status/order.
StateManager.updateEpic appends an EPIC_UPDATED activity event with bounded changed fields and epic context.
Activity logging errors remain non-blocking and follow existing error handling conventions.
get_activity_log and WebSocket activity log show epic create/update events in reverse chronological order like other events.
Regression tests cover create/update activity entries and ensure existing EPIC_CREATED/EPIC_UPDATED WebSocket broadcasts still occur.

implementationPlan.description: Extend StateManager activity append support so epic-only events can include epicId. Either add an optional epic parameter to appendActivity/appendActivityAsync or construct the ActivityEvent with an explicit epicId while preserving existing task/worker/proposal behavior and error handling.
implementationPlan.note: Extended appendActivity/appendActivityAsync to accept optional Epic context and set top-level epicId for epic-only events while preserving existing task/worker/proposal behavior. schema.ts already had ActivityEvent.epicId and EPIC_CREATED/EPIC_UPDATED types, so no schema edit was needed. Added failing regression tests first and confirmed they failed before the fix; focused green check: 3 files / 4 tests passed for epic lifecycle activity.

implementationPlan.description: Call activity logging from createEpic and updateEpic. Payloads should be bounded: include title/status/order on create and only changed safe fields on update, with long text truncated or summarized. Do not log full unbounded architectureNotes.
implementationPlan.note: createEpic now logs EPIC_CREATED with top-level epicId and bounded payload {epicId,title,status,order}; updateEpic logs EPIC_UPDATED with top-level epicId, changedFields, safe scalar values, description preview/length, architectureNotes length only, and epicRails count only. Activity logging remains fire-and-forget/non-blocking through existing appendActivity error handling. Focused check: StateManager epic activity tests passed (1 file / 2 tests).

implementationPlan.description: Add regression tests that create/update an epic, flush activity if needed, read getActivityLog, and assert EPIC_CREATED/EPIC_UPDATED include epicId and bounded payloads. Preserve existing WebSocket/StateManager emit tests.
implementationPlan.note: Added regression tests that failed before implementation: StateManager create/update epic activity entries with epicId and bounded payloads; get_activity_log epicId/eventType filtering with newest-first EPIC_UPDATED then EPIC_CREATED; WebSocket GET_ACTIVITY_LOG returning the same epic lifecycle ordering. Existing EPIC_CREATED/EPIC_UPDATED emit assertions remain in place. Green check: StateManager/tools/WebSocketServer targeted suites passed (3 files / 291 tests).

implementationPlan.description: Run packages/moe-daemon npm test and npm run build; verify activity log parsing validation still accepts epic-only events and handles malformed log lines gracefully.
implementationPlan.note: Final validation complete. npm run build passed (tsc ok). Full daemon test suite passed: 25 files / 478 tests. git diff --check on task-relevant files returned only CRLF normalization warnings. Adversarial review: activity writes remain non-blocking via existing appendActivity catch/finally, task/proposal/worker behavior preserved by appending optional Epic as the last parameter, epic-only events have top-level epicId and no taskId, bounded payloads avoid full architectureNotes/descriptions/rails, and existing validateActivityEvent already treats epicId/taskId/workerId as optional so epic-only parsing is accepted while malformed JSON remains skipped via existing catch/log path.

comments.author: architect-2992
comments.content: Token-budget check: epic activity events should log bounded fields/previews/counts only. Do not log full architectureNotes, descriptions, rails arrays, or unbounded update payloads.

planningNotes.approachesConsidered: Rejected logging full architectureNotes/epicRails on every update because payloads can be large. Prefer bounded changed fields or counts/previews for audit usefulness without log bloat.
planningNotes.codebaseInsights: Task create/update/delete and epic delete append activity. Epic create/update currently only emit WebSocket events. ActivityEvent supports optional epicId without taskId.
planningNotes.risks: appendActivity accepts an optional task but not an epic object, so worker may need to extend it or manually pass epicId. Ensure validationActivityEvent accepts epicId-only events. Include error handling and tests.
